verification.target_vocab_notes: 帽子 (ぼうし) appears clearly at the beginning of the sentence as the object of とってください
verification.kana_notes: Perfect match between kana and Japanese sentence
verification.grammar_notes: Uses N5 grammar: を particle, te-form (とって) + ください (polite request)
verification.difficulty_notes: Grammar: Simple te-form + ください pattern (3/10). Vocabulary: Only basic words (3/10). Length: Short sentence (2/10). Cultural: Universal concept (2/10). Overall: Low-mid N5
verification.overall_notes: Clean example with clear target vocabulary, accurate kana, and appropriate N5 grammar for polite requests

verification.target_vocab_notes: 帽子 (ぼうし) appears clearly in the sentence as the object being desired
verification.kana_notes: Error in kana: 欲しがっている should be ほしがっている not ほっしがっている (small tsu っ should not be there)
verification.grammar_notes: Contains ている form (欲しがっている) which is continuous/progressive aspect. While borderline, がっている is N4+ grammar expressing visible desire in third person
verification.difficulty_notes: Grammar: Uses N4+ がっている pattern (8/10). Vocabulary: Multiple words including あたらしい (5/10). Length: Medium (5/10). Cultural: Universal (2/10). Overall: Too advanced
verification.overall_notes: Contains N4+ grammar (がっている form) and has a kana error. Not suitable for N5

verification.target_vocab_notes: 帽子 (ぼうし) appears clearly in the sentence as the object being shown
verification.kana_notes: Perfect match between kana and Japanese sentence
verification.grammar_notes: Uses N5 grammar: は particle, に particle (indirect object), を particle, ta-form (見せた)
verification.difficulty_notes: Grammar: Simple past tense with に particle for indirect object (5/10). Vocabulary: Multiple N5 words including 私たち, きれい (5/10). Length: Medium sentence (5/10). Cultural: Universal (2/10). Overall: Mid N5
verification.overall_notes: Good N5 example with clear vocabulary, accurate kana, and appropriate grammar

verification.target_vocab_notes: ぼうし appears in kana form in the sentence as the object to be removed

verification.grammar_notes: Uses N5 grammar: この + noun, では (location marker), を particle, とる (dictionary form 取る) in te-form + ください
verification.difficulty_notes: Grammar: Te-form + ください with では (4/10). Vocabulary: この, 部屋, では construction (5/10). Length: Medium (5/10). Cultural: Universal concept (2/10). Overall: Mid N5
verification.overall_notes: Good N5 example demonstrating polite requests in specific locations. Target vocab in kana form is acceptable

verification.target_vocab_notes: じょうぶ appears in kana form as a na-adjective predicate

verification.difficulty_notes: Grammar: Complex comparison structure with と同じように (7/10). Vocabulary: Multiple words including 前, 同じ (6/10). Length: Medium-long (6/10). Cultural: Universal (2/10). Overall: Advanced N5
verification.overall_notes: While using only N5 patterns, the と同じように construction is quite advanced for beginners. Consider for upper N5 learners

verification.overall_notes: Contains N4 grammar (そうだ for appearance). Not suitable for N5

verification.target_vocab_notes: 手に入れた (te ni ireta) is a compound expression meaning 'obtained/acquired'. The target vocab 入れる appears but as part of this set phrase rather than with its basic meaning 'to put in'
verification.kana_notes: Perfect match between kana and Japanese sentence
verification.grammar_notes: Uses N5 grammar: は particle, の (possessive), 欲しい (want), は (topic), なんでも (anything), を particle, ta-form
verification.difficulty_notes: Grammar: Complex sentence with relative clause structure (7/10). Vocabulary: 君, 欲しい, なんでも, compound expression (7/10). Length: Medium-long (6/10). Cultural: Universal (2/10). Overall: Advanced N5
verification.overall_notes: Target vocabulary appears in compound 手に入れる rather than standalone meaning. May not effectively teach the base meaning 'to put in'. Advanced sentence structure

verification.target_vocab_notes: いれています appears showing the continuous action of making coffee
verification.kana_notes: Perfect match between kana and Japanese sentence
verification.grammar_notes: Contains ている form (いれています) which is continuous aspect. While basic usage, ている is borderline and often considered N4+ for formal teaching

verification.overall_notes: Uses ている form which is borderline N5/N4. If accepting very basic ている usage, this could work. Otherwise reject

verification.target_vocab_notes: 遠からず (tōkarazu) means 'before long/soon' - this is a set expression. While it contains 遠, it doesn't demonstrate the basic meaning 'far' but rather an idiomatic usage

verification.overall_notes: Target vocabulary appears in idiomatic expression 遠からず rather than demonstrating basic meaning 'far'. Not suitable for teaching the word

verification.target_vocab_notes: によると appears meaning 'according to' which is a set phrase. Target vocabulary is 夜 (よる - evening/night). The よる in によると is the particle によって (according to) in its と-form, NOT the word for night. False positive
verification.kana_notes: Error: こんにちは should be きょう (今日 - today), not こんにちは (hello greeting). The reading is completely wrong
verification.grammar_notes: Contains らしい which is hearsay/seeming grammar (N4+). Also uses によると which while useful is N4+
verification.difficulty_notes: Grammar: N4+ によると and らしい (8/10). Vocabulary: Multiple words (6/10). Length: Medium-long (6/10). Cultural: Universal (2/10). Overall: N4 level
verification.overall_notes: Multiple problems: target vocab doesn't appear (によると is not 夜), kana error (こんにちは should be きょう), and N4+ grammar (によると, らしい)

verification.target_vocab_notes: によると (according to) is a set phrase where よる is the verb よる (to be based on), NOT 夜 (よる - evening). False positive from substring matching
verification.kana_notes: Error: こんにちは should be きょう (今日 - today). The reading is wrong - こんにちは is the greeting 'hello', not the word for 'today'
verification.grammar_notes: Contains によると which is N4+ grammar (according to). Uses によると quotation/source pattern
verification.difficulty_notes: Grammar: N4+ によると construction (7/10). Vocabulary: Multiple words (5/10). Length: Medium (5/10). Cultural: Universal (2/10). Overall: N4 level
verification.overall_notes: Multiple problems: target vocab 夜 doesn't appear (によると is different word), kana error (こんにちは should be きょう), and N4+ grammar

verification.kana_notes: Perfect match between kana and Japanese sentence
verification.grammar_notes: Uses N5 grammar: に particle (time), どこか (somewhere), へ particle, ta-form (行きました), か question
verification.difficulty_notes: Grammar: Past tense question (5/10). Vocabulary: 夏休み, どこか (5/10). Length: Medium (4/10). Cultural: Universal (2/10). Overall: Mid N5
verification.overall_notes: Good N5 example. Clear and practical for learners

verification.target_vocab_notes: どこ appears in どこか (somewhere)
verification.kana_notes: Perfect match between kana and Japanese sentence
verification.grammar_notes: Uses N5 grammar: この + noun, は particle, どこか (somewhere), へ particle, 行く + のですか explanatory question
verification.difficulty_notes: Grammar: のですか explanatory question (6/10). Vocabulary: この, 夏休み, どこか (5/10). Length: Medium (5/10). Cultural: Universal (2/10). Overall: Mid-upper N5
verification.overall_notes: のですか is borderline advanced for N5. Otherwise good example

verification.target_vocab_notes: 暇 (ひま) appears in kana form as the predicate
verification.kana_notes: Perfect match between kana and Japanese sentence
verification.grammar_notes: Uses N5 grammar: 今晩 (tonight), na-adjective (暇), かな question particle (casual wondering)
verification.difficulty_notes: Grammar: Casual かな question (4/10). Vocabulary: 今晩, ひま (4/10). Length: Very short (2/10). Cultural: Universal (2/10). Overall: Low-mid N5
verification.overall_notes: Good simple N5 example showing casual question form

verification.target_vocab_notes: 暇 (ひま) appears with polite お prefix (お暇)
verification.kana_notes: Perfect match between kana and Japanese sentence
verification.grammar_notes: Uses N5 grammar: 明日 (tomorrow), の (possessive), 夕方 (evening), は particle, お + adjective (polite), です, か question
verification.difficulty_notes: Grammar: Polite question with お prefix (5/10). Vocabulary: 明日, 夕方, polite form (5/10). Length: Medium (4/10). Cultural: Japanese politeness (4/10). Overall: Mid N5
verification.overall_notes: Good N5 example showing polite form with お prefix. Appropriate for mid-level learners

verification.target_vocab_notes: 暇 (ひま) appears in kana form as the predicate
verification.kana_notes: Perfect match between kana and Japanese sentence
verification.grammar_notes: Uses N5 grammar: は particle, 明日 (tomorrow), na-adjective (暇), でしょう (probably/polite presumption)
verification.difficulty_notes: Grammar: でしょう presumptive (5/10). Vocabulary: 彼, 明日, ひま (4/10). Length: Short (3/10). Cultural: Universal (2/10). Overall: Mid N5
verification.overall_notes: Good N5 example showing presumptive でしょう form

verification.target_vocab_notes: ひま appears twice in the sentence (once with は and once as predicate)
verification.kana_notes: Error: こんにちは should be きょう (今日 - today). This is reading the kanji 今日 as the greeting こんにちは instead of きょう
verification.grammar_notes: Uses N5 grammar: は particle (doubled for emphasis), 今日 (today), na-adjective + です
verification.difficulty_notes: Grammar: Simple statement (3/10). Vocabulary: 今日, ひま (3/10). Length: Short (3/10). Cultural: Universal (2/10). Overall: Low-mid N5
verification.overall_notes: Good simple example but has kana error. こんにちは should be きょう (today). Needs correction

verification.target_vocab_notes: ひま appears twice in the sentence (once in each clause)
verification.kana_notes: Perfect match between kana and Japanese sentence
verification.grammar_notes: Uses N5 grammar: 今 (now), は particle, い-adjective (忙しい), が (but), 明日 (tomorrow), になる (become), だろう (probably), と (quotation), 言った (said). While complex, all elements are N5
verification.difficulty_notes: Grammar: Complex sentence with multiple clauses, quotation, になる, だろう (9/10). Vocabulary: Multiple words and verb conjugations (8/10). Length: Long (8/10). Cultural: Universal (2/10). Overall: Upper N5 limit
verification.overall_notes: Very complex sentence structure at the absolute upper limit of N5. Multiple clauses with quotation. May be too difficult for most N5 learners

verification.target_vocab_notes: ひま appears as the predicate in casual question form
verification.kana_notes: Perfect match between kana and Japanese sentence
verification.grammar_notes: Uses N5 grammar: 明日 (tomorrow), の (possessive), 晩 (evening), は particle, na-adjective casual question (omitting copula)
verification.difficulty_notes: Grammar: Casual question omitting copula (4/10). Vocabulary: 明日, 晩 (4/10). Length: Very short (2/10). Cultural: Universal (2/10). Overall: Low-mid N5
verification.overall_notes: Good casual N5 example showing informal speech patterns

verification.target_vocab_notes: 鳴く (なく) appears clearly as the verb showing bird sounds
verification.kana_notes: Perfect match between kana and Japanese sentence
verification.grammar_notes: Uses N5 grammar: は particle, dictionary form verb (鳴く)
verification.difficulty_notes: Grammar: Simple subject-verb statement (2/10). Vocabulary: Basic words only (2/10). Length: Very short (1/10). Cultural: Universal (2/10). Overall: Beginner N5
verification.overall_notes: Excellent simple N5 example. Perfect for beginners to understand basic sentence structure and the verb
